headline: OpenAI Announcements Make Waves in Stock Market It Has Shunned
summary: Just last week, OpenAI sent shares of e-commerce companies Shopify Inc. and Etsy Inc. soaring after unveiling an instant buy option in ChatGPT.  Then a blog post detailing new features the company is using internally sent a fresh wave of jitters through software stocks like Atlassian Corp., already reeling from fears about AI disruption.  Such market-moving sway, usually reserved for behemoths like Apple Inc. or Nvidia Corp., is the latest sign of OpenAI’s rising influence on a wide variety of stocks even though there’s little indication the startup, which was recently valued at $500 billion, plans to list its shares anytime soon.

headline: Northbeam Launches Industry-First Attribution Model That Connects Ad Views and Clicks Across Platforms to Real Revenue
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, October 07, 2025--Northbeam, the leader in marketing attribution, today launched its new Clicks + Deterministic Views attribution model, giving marketers a complete picture of how campaigns drive purchases. The first-of-its-kind solution, developed in direct partnership with Meta, TikTok, Snapchat, Pinterest, Axon, MNTN, and Vibe, unifies in-app clicks and ad views across platforms and ties them directly to first-party transaction data.

headline: A Tech Rebound Lifts U.S. Stocks, and the March to Record Highs Resumes
summary: A rebound in tech stocks helped lift major indexes Wednesday, with investors quickly casting aside concerns about the profitability of artificial-intelligence investments.  On Tuesday, a report in The Information suggested that Oracle’s business renting specialized servers powered by  Nvidia  chips wasn’t particularly profitable.  Overall, the S&P 500 gained 0.6% to close at a record high for a 33rd time this year.
